task_completion.description: Load the sample order lines dataset in Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Oracle Analytics Desktop is open and the dataset (e.g., sample_order_lines2023) is visible in the data panel or loaded into a new workbook.
The visualization canvas shows a Treemap chart type selected (nested rectangles) in the grammar/properties panel.

task_completion.description: Group the treemap by Product Category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Product Category' is visible in the 'Category' or 'Group By' drop zone of the visualization configuration panel.

task_completion.description: Group the treemap by Product Sub Category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Product Sub Category' is visible in the 'Category' or 'Group By' drop zone, hierarchically under 'Product Category'.

task_completion.description: Determine rectangle sizes by Revenue (or Sales).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A metric for 'Revenue' (or 'Sales', due to dataset naming) is placed in the 'Size' drop zone of the visualization configuration panel.

task_completion.description: Save the workbook as 'revenue_treemap' in the Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the filename 'revenue_treemap' and the destination 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents', or the workbook tab is named 'revenue_treemap'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to create the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface, dragging and dropping columns, rather than using a terminal or text editor to write a file.

integrity.description: The treemap visualization is genuinely rendered in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The canvas displays an actual rendered treemap with rectangles of varying sizes, not just an empty configuration.

integrity.description: The agent used Oracle Analytics Desktop, not a different spreadsheet or charting tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window clearly belongs to Oracle Analytics Desktop, matching its distinct UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is 'revenue_treemap.dva' saved in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents'. The visualization must be a treemap. Note: The actual dataset file on the desktop is 'sample_order_lines2023.xlsx' (not 'sample_order_lines.xlsx'). Additionally, the dataset contains a 'Sales' column instead of 'Revenue', so the agent using 'Sales' for the sizing requirement is acceptable and expected. The final file must be at least 5KB.
